checked_dimensions.name: spec_compliance
checked_dimensions.evidence: The work follows issue #255 and the contract: v1 governance artifacts were created, Claude Code was launched first for a separate local specbridge-v2 MVP, and Codex captured verification evidence.

checked_dimensions.name: acceptance_criteria
checked_dimensions.evidence: The v2 workspace exists locally with README, AGENTS, context files, bootstrap execution contract, scope manifest, final report fixture, PowerShell validator, and tests. Claude reported 33 passing tests and Codex independently reran scripts/test.ps1 with 33 passed and exit code 0.

checked_dimensions.name: policy_boundaries
checked_dimensions.evidence: No v2 GitHub repository was created, no external v2 repository mutation occurred, no dependencies were installed, and no production, billing, auth, database, CI/CD workflow, deployment, hosted runtime, mutation-capable MCP, cleanup enforcement, retention enforcement, or Qwen integration work occurred.

checked_dimensions.name: security_rules
checked_dimensions.evidence: The v2 AGENTS and v1 contract both block secrets, network requests, remotes, package installation, provider configuration, production, deployment, auth, billing, and CI/CD changes. Codex also scanned v2 files for non-ASCII text and found none.

checked_dimensions.name: changed_file_scope
checked_dimensions.evidence: All v1 changed files are declared in the issue-255 scope manifest. The sibling v2 workspace is outside v1 scope validation by design and is recorded in the contract and runtime evidence as the explicitly authorized local workspace.

checked_dimensions.name: test_evidence
checked_dimensions.evidence: v1 validate-contracts and validate-contract-scopes passed before v2 launch. v2 scripts/test.ps1 passed with 33 assertions, v2 git status was clean, and v1 final-report/audit/smoke validations are recorded in the final report.

checked_dimensions.name: ci_evidence
checked_dimensions.evidence: No v1 PR or GitHub CI run has been opened for branch codex/issue-255-serious-product-build-pilot, and v2 remains a local-only repository without remote CI. Merge remains blocked until the operator opens a PR and CI/review gates pass.

checked_dimensions.name: final_report_honesty
checked_dimensions.evidence: The final report states local completion, records that v2 is local only, notes that v1 cannot enforce sibling paths through scope validation, and explicitly says the v1 work is not merged and deployment is blocked.

findings.evidence: The local pilot is complete, but no v1 PR or GitHub CI evidence exists yet for this branch.
findings.recommendation: Open a v1 PR for branch codex/issue-255-serious-product-build-pilot and require GitHub CI plus review before merge.

findings.evidence: The v2 implementation lives in a local sibling repository. v1 validators can validate v1 evidence but cannot directly enforce the sibling workspace file list.
findings.recommendation: If v2 becomes a durable product repository, create a remote repo and add its own CI under a dedicated contract.

unresolved_risks: No GitHub PR or CI exists yet for the v1 branch.
The v2 repository is local only and has no remote backup or CI.
v1 scope validation cannot directly enforce sibling repository paths.
